title: Encoders, Decoders, and Multiplexers
summary: These combinational circuits handle data routing and encoding: encoders compress inputs to a binary code, decoders expand a binary code to one active output, multiplexers select one of many inputs.
content_html:
Encoders

An encoder converts an active input signal into a compact binary output code. If it has n input lines, it produces at most log₂(n) output bits.

Priority Encoder: When multiple inputs are active, it encodes the highest-priority input and ignores lower-priority ones.

4-to-2 Priority Encoder Example

I3I2I1I0Output Y1Output Y0
000100
001x01
01xx10
1xxx11

Applications: Keyboards (key press → ASCII code), interrupt priority systems.

Decoders

A decoder converts an n-bit binary input into 2ⁿ one-hot outputs (exactly one output HIGH at a time).

2-to-4 Decoder Truth Table

A1A0Y0Y1Y2Y3
001000
010100
100010
110001

Applications: Memory chip select (address decoder), 7-segment display drivers.

Multiplexers (MUX)

A multiplexer selects one of 2ⁿ data inputs and routes it to a single output, controlled by n select lines.

2-to-1 MUX: Y = D0·S̄ + D1·S
Select SOutput Y
0D0
1D1

Applications: Routing data in CPUs, connecting ALU output to registers, parallel-to-serial conversion.

Demultiplexers (DEMUX)

The inverse of a MUX. Routes one input to one of 2ⁿ outputs.

ComponentDirectionSelect lines for n outputs
MUXMany → Onelog₂(inputs)
DEMUXOne → Manylog₂(outputs)
EncoderOne-hot → BinaryN/A
DecoderBinary → One-hotN/A

exercises.title: Encoder output bits
exercises.prompt: A 4-to-2 priority encoder has how many output bits?

exercises.title: Decoder outputs
exercises.prompt: A 3-to-8 decoder produces how many outputs?

exercises.title: Decoder one-hot
exercises.prompt: In a decoder, at any time exactly how many outputs are HIGH?

exercises.title: MUX direction
exercises.options_json: ["One input to many outputs", "Many inputs to one output", "Binary to one-hot", "One-hot to binary"]

exercises.title: MUX select lines
exercises.prompt: A 4-to-1 MUX requires how many select lines?

exercises.title: Priority encoder
exercises.prompt: In a priority encoder, when multiple inputs are active:
exercises.options_json: ["All are encoded simultaneously", "The lowest priority is encoded", "The highest priority is encoded", "The output is undefined"]

exercises.title: 2-to-4 decoder A1=1,A0=0
exercises.prompt: In a 2-to-4 decoder with inputs A1=1, A0=0, which output is HIGH?

exercises.title: DEMUX
exercises.options_json: ["Many inputs to one output", "One input to one of many outputs", "Binary to one-hot", "One-hot to binary"]
exercises.answer: One input to one of many outputs

exercises.title: Keyboard encoder
exercises.prompt: A keyboard uses an encoder to:
exercises.options_json: ["Decode key presses", "Map each key press to a binary ASCII code", "Select which key to illuminate", "Control key repeat rate"]
exercises.answer: Map each key press to a binary ASCII code

exercises.title: Memory address decoder
exercises.prompt: A memory address decoder in a computer system:
exercises.options_json: ["Encodes the CPU instructions", "Selects which memory chip to activate based on address bits", "Decodes ASCII from keyboard", "Routes data from RAM to CPU"]
exercises.answer: Selects which memory chip to activate based on address bits
